no-privelege: У вас нет прав администратора для установки этого параметра.
not-enough-stock: На складе не осталось достаточного количества товаров для этой покупки.
no-price: Этот товар еще не имеет цены
no-product: В этом слоте ничего не продается
no-register-space: В реестре магазина недостаточно места для получения оплаты
purchased-zero-quantity: Вы должны приобрети не менее 1 предмета за раз.
not-enough-money: У вас недостаточно средств, для покупки этого предмета
not-registered-with-shop: Эта витрина еще не связана с кассой. Невозможно совершить покупку.
couldnt-find-register: Не удалось найти кассу магазина – она в незагруженной зоне?
item-cost: Этот товар стоит {0}x {1}. Пожалуйста, возьмите в руку средства для оплаты товара
no-gacha-ball: No gacha ball provided in input slot
gacha-atleast-one: You need to bundle a gacha ball with atleast one item
gacha-bundled-zero: You must bundle atleast one gacha ball
ledger-already-set: This ledger already recording sales for a shop
ledger-shop-not-found: The Shop associated with this ledger no longer exists
ledger-not-set: This ledger is not set to any shop. Sneak-Right-Click a register to link it
ledger-set: Ledger set to retrieve sales from {0}
handbooktext-register: Allows you to set up your own personal shop. Any proceeds from a given mat, crate, clothing stall, or sculpture stall linked to this shop will go directly to this register's inventory. Be sure to empty out the register from time to time, as you will not be able to make any sales if it is full. If you want to keep track of all sales for your shop, you can craft a ledger and link it to this stall in order to view the sales on a Year/Month basis. The Register can also create a global, publicly visible waypoint by enabling the tick box in the Register's menu. If enabled, your register's location will be broadcasted so other players can find your shop easier.
handbooktext-tellermachine: Allows your customers to exchange currency between two types. The currency conversion is done directly from your register's inventory, so it may only happen if you have enough items to support the trade. Note that these trades are bidirectional, so do not set up conversions which you do not consider equivalent. As an example 10 Copper Currency might be worth 1 Silver Currency. Such a trade would allow players to exchange either 10 Copper for 1 Silver, or 1 Silver for 10 Copper.
handbooktext-stall: Allows you to sell a number of items to players for purchase. In order to function, it must be linked to a register in order to function properly. Any goods sold by this stall will have its proceeds delievered straight to the register's inventory.
handbooktext-gacha: Allows you to sell chance-based goods to players for purchase. The gacha machine must be filled with gacha balls which can be filled in a gacha loader. In order to function, it must be linked to a register in order to function properly. Any goods sold by this stall will have its proceeds delievered straight to the register's inventory.
handbooktext-gachaloader: Allows you to fill gacha balls with items to be used in a gacha stall. Each ball can be filled with up to 6 different stacks of items.
handbooktext-clothingstand: Allows you to sell a number of clothing items to players for purchase. In order to function, it must be linked to a register in order to function properly. Any goods sold by this stall will have its proceeds delievered straight to the register's inventory.
handbooktext-weaponrack: Allows you to sell a up to two types of weapons or tools and a shield to players for purchase. In order to function, it must be linked to a register in order to function properly. Any goods sold by this stall will have its proceeds delievered straight to the register's inventory.
handbooktext-toolrack: Allows you to sell a number of tools to players for purchase. In order to function, it must be linked to a register in order to function properly. Any goods sold by this stall will have its proceeds delievered straight to the register's inventory.
handbooktext-tenretni: Allows you to read third-party data from the internet (Right now, just Pastebin.com) by providing its URL ID
